title: EVPN Type-2 MAC Mobility: Sequence Escalation, Move Storms, Duplicate MAC, and Silent Drift Across VTEPs
exclusions: BGP **EVPN** **withdrawal**-velocity storms dominated by unmatched **UPDATE** **withdraw** tallies without **Type-2** **mobility** semantics (see **UC-18.3.18**); **VXLAN** **VTEP** tunnel reachability and generic overlay neighbor health without per-**MAC** **ownership** math (see **UC-18.3.4**); **Ethernet** **Segment** **DF** **election** churn and **BUM** stability (see **UC-18.3.21**); **host-route** / **ARP** scale headroom and **RIB** occupancy (see **UC-18.3.20**); **Cisco** **ACI** **APIC** fabrics (UC-18.1.x series).
dataSources: `index=dc_fabric` / `index=network_ops` / `index=sdn_ops` / `index=os` / `index=cisco_dc` (rename per governance) joining five active **`sourcetype`** lanes: **`sdn:evpn:route`** (**JSON** **BGP** **EVPN** **NLRI** snapshots for **Type-2** **MAC**/**IP** with **RFC** **7432** **MAC** **Mobility** extended-community **sequence** extracted into **`mm_seq`**), **`sdn:bgp:neighbor`** (peer **State**/**Established** transitions correlated to **move** bursts), **`sdn:vtep:inventory`** (**loopback**-to-**`leaf_hostname`** CMDB parity for **VTEP** attribution), **`sdn:fabric:config`** (**`evi`**/**`vni`** naming, **`rt`** import/export drift markers), **`arista:eos:syslog`** as the reference syslog lane where **`EVPN-MACMOVE`**/**`BGP-5-ADJCHANGE`** lines land—**Heavy** **Forwarder** **`props`** clones may map **`cisco:nxos:syslog`** (**`L2RIB-3-L2RIB_THREAD_MAC_MOVE_DETECTED`**, **Splunkbase** **6876**), **`juniper:junos:syslog`** (**`RPD_EVPN_MAC_MOVE`**, **2847**), **SONiC** **`docker`** **FRR** **`zebra`** mac-move strings, and **Cumulus** **FRR** logs into the **same** **EVENTTYPE** macros.
app: **Splunk Add-on for Cisco IOS/NX-OS** ([Splunkbase **2846**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2846)); **Splunk Add-on for Cisco Nexus** ([Splunkbase **6876**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6876)); **Splunk Add-on for Arista EOS** ([Splunkbase **4495**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4495)); **Splunk Add-on for Juniper Networks** ([Splunkbase **2847**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2847)); optional **Splunk Connect for Syslog** for scaled fan-in; bespoke collectors landing **`sdn:*`** structured routes from **eAPI**/**NETCONF**/**gNMI**/**vtysh** JSON.
spl:
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=sdn_ops OR index=os OR index=cisco_dc)
(sourcetype="sdn:evpn:route" OR sourcetype="sdn:bgp:neighbor" OR sourcetype="sdn:vtep:inventory" OR sourcetype="sdn:fabric:config" OR sourcetype="arista:eos:syslog")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval leaf_node=trim(coalesce(leaf_hostname,hostname,switch_name,device_name,host,""))
| eval mac_norm=upper(trim(replace(coalesce(mac_address,client_mac,sticky_mac,dl_src,evpn_mac,""),"[:-]","")))
| eval vni_key=tonumber(coalesce(vni,vxlan_vni,l2_vni,evi_id,evi,""))
| eval mm_seq=tonumber(coalesce(mobility_seq,mm_seq,mac_mobility_seq,seq_num,route_seq,""))
| eval vtep_ip=trim(coalesce(originating_router,originator_ip,nexthop,nexthop_vtep,remote_vtep,vtep_src,nve_src,""))
| eval evt_family=case(
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(sticky|duplicate.?mac|dup.?mac|aliasing)"),"dup_mac_signal",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(L2RIB-3-L2RIB_THREAD_MAC_MOVE|EVPN-MACMOVE|RPD_EVPN_MAC_MOVE|MAC move|mac.?move)"),"syslog_mac_move",
    match(lower(coalesce(route_action,evpn_op,nlri_op,"")),"(?i)withdraw|wdr|purge"),"rt2_withdraw",
    match(lower(coalesce(route_action,evpn_op,nlri_op,"")),"(?i)add|advertise|learn"),"rt2_advertise",
    match(lower(coalesce(route_action,evpn_op,nlri_op,"")),"(?i)update|modify|replace"),"rt2_update",
    sourcetype=="sdn:evpn:route" AND match(lower(coalesce(evpn_route_type,nlri_rt,bgp_evpn_rt,"")),"(?i)type.?2|mac.?ip|^2$"),"rt2_structured",
    null())
| where isnotnull(evt_family) OR mm_seq>0 OR match(lower(_raw),"(?i)type.?2|mac.?ip|l2vpn.?evpn")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count AS mac_evt dc(vtep_ip) AS vtep_dc max(mm_seq) AS seq_top values(vtep_ip) AS vtep_list values(evt_family) AS evt_mix values(leaf_node) AS leaf_list min(_time) AS win_start max(_time) AS win_end BY _time vni_key mac_norm
| eval primary_vtep=mvindex(mvsort(mvdedup(vtep_list)),0)
| eval ownership_spray=if(vtep_dc>1 AND mac_norm!="","multi_vtep_same_mac","single_owner_window")
| eval seq_stress=if(seq_top>=5 OR mac_evt>=25 OR ownership_spray=="multi_vtep_same_mac",1,0)
| eval storm_bucket=case(
    seq_stress=1 AND match(mvjoin(evt_mix,"|"),"(?i)dup_mac_signal"),"dup_mac_investigate",
    seq_stress=1 AND mac_evt>=60,"vm_migration_storm",
    seq_stress=1 AND seq_top>=5,"mobility_seq_runaway",
    seq_stress=1,"mac_churn_anomaly",
    null())
| where isnotnull(storm_bucket)
| eventstats sum(mac_evt) AS vni_churn_sum BY _time vni_key
| eval vni_pressure=if(vni_churn_sum>600,"vni_hot","vni_nominal")
| lookup sdn_vtep_switch_map ip AS primary_vtep OUTPUTNEW site_code platform_role expected_owner
| sort - mac_evt - seq_top - vni_churn_sum
| head 350
| table _time vni_key mac_norm mac_evt vtep_dc seq_top storm_bucket ownership_spray vni_pressure vni_churn_sum evt_mix leaf_list win_start win_end site_code primary_vtep
description: Monitors **EVPN** **Route** **Type-2** (**MAC**/**IP**) **advertisement** churn tied to **RFC** **7432** **§15.1** **MAC** **mobility** procedure: **new** learn, **relocate** between **VTEPs**, **delete**/**withdraw**, **sticky**/**duplicate** **MAC** diagnostics, and **mobility** **sequence** **number** escalation whenever a **host** **MAC** reattaches on a different **next-hop** **router**. Surfaces **VNI**-level **MAC** move rates, **per**-**VTEP** **ownership** flips, bursts consistent with **VM** migration waves, and **silent** workload **drift** when **hypervisor**/**orchestrator** tickets omit moves Splunk already sees from **BGP** **logs**. Distinguishes **control-plane** **mobility** narratives from raw **withdrawal** throughput (**UC-18.3.18**), overlay tunnel health (**UC-18.3.4**), **ESI** **DF** stability (**UC-18.3.21**), and **scale** headroom (**UC-18.3.20**).
value: Cuts time-to-root-cause when east-west drops trace to **MAC** **reachability** oscillation: sequence jumps isolate **flapping** **NICs** or **duplicate** **guest** images, **VNI** **heat** maps justify **tenant** **isolation** or **broadcast** **domain** rightsizing, and **orchestration** **gaps** (**moves** without **ServiceNow**/**vCenter** **tasks**) shorten audits of **shadow** **migration** activity. Capacity planners gain defensible charts of **mobility** **tax** on **leaf** **CPUs** before **refresh** **cycles**, while **NOC** handoffs carry **storm_bucket** labels (**dup_mac_investigate**, **vm_migration_storm**, **mobility_seq_runaway**) that map cleanly to runbooks without reopening vendor **CLI** sessions.
implementation: Normalize **`sdn:evpn:route`** from **show** **bgp** **l2vpn** **evpn** / **show** **evpn** **mac-table** collectors; ingest **`arista:eos:syslog`** (or **`props`**-aliased **NX-OS**/**Junos**/**FRR** lanes); refresh **`sdn:vtep:inventory`** nightly; publish **`evpn_mac_mobility_core`** saved search; maintain **`sdn_vtep_switch_map`** **CSV**; throttle alerts per **`mac_norm`**+**`vni_key`**; attach **`CHG`** overlays for approved **live** **migration** windows.
visualization: (1) **Timechart** — **`mac_evt`** stacked by **`storm_bucket`** per **`vni_key`**. (2) **Heat-map** — **`leaf_node`** × **`vni_pressure`**. (3) **Scatter** — **`seq_top`** vs **`vtep_dc`** highlighting **`ownership_spray`**. (4) **Table** — top **`mac_norm`** rows with **`evt_mix`** and **`vni_churn_sum`**. (5) **Single-value** tiles for count of **`dup_mac_investigate`** vs **`vm_migration_storm`**.
schema: CIM **Network_Traffic** (**BGP**/**EVPN** update proxies, tunnel endpoint fields when **VTEP** IPs appear) plus **Inventory** (**MAC**/**VNI**/**sequence** posture tied to **`sdn:vtep:inventory`** entities).
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **Inventory** when **`sdn:vtep:inventory`** refreshes stabilize; keep **`Network_Traffic`**-oriented **`sdn:evpn:route`** raw lanes unaccelerated until field schemas freeze.

references.title: IETF RFC 7432 — BGP MPLS-Based Ethernet VPN (MAC Mobility §15.1)
IETF RFC 8365 — Network Virtualization Overlay Using EVPN
Cisco Nexus 9000 NX-OS BGP EVPN Configuration Guide
NVIDIA Cumulus Linux — Ethernet Virtual Private Network (EVPN)

knownFalsePositives:
**vmotion_batch_window** — Clustered **VMware** **vSphere** **DRS** sweeps deliberately relocate dozens of **MACs** per minute while **BGP** remains **Established**; align Splunk **`storm_bucket`** **`vm_migration_storm`** positives with **CMDB** **`vmotion_batch_window`** tokens tied to **maintenance** **`CHG`** records before treating mobility as fabric misconfiguration.

**evpn_rt2_mass_relearn** — Shortening **MAC** **aging** or toggling **ARP** **suppression** forces **Type-2** mass **re-advertise** cycles resembling move bursts—annotate **`evpn_rt2_mass_relearn`** runbook suppressions referencing **Nexus** / **EOS** knob changes documented in the same **CAB** evening.

**anycast_gateway_rebalance** — **Anycast** **SVI** migrations shift **gateway** **VTEP** sourcing without bad **NICs**—pair syslog **`dup_mac_signal`** crumbs with **`anycast_gateway_rebalance`** tickets when first-hop relays reshuffle during **FHRP** choreography lacking true duplicate hosts.

**kubernetes_cni_sandbox_shuffle** — **Container** **CNI** rebuilds recreate **sandbox** **MACs** rapidly on **bare-metal** **Kubernetes** nodes attached as **CEs**—downgrade **`mac_churn_anomaly`** when **`kubernetes_cni_sandbox_shuffle`** automation IDs describe **namespace** drains sans L2 loops.

**storage_live_migration_noise** — **Hypervisor** **storage-only** migrations sometimes pulse **control-plane** **Type-2** traffic without IP moves—use **`storage_live_migration_noise`** tagging when **vCenter** tasks list **storage** **vMotion** exclusively and Splunk **`vtep_dc`** stays **1**.

**storm_control_mac_suppression** — **Storm-control** **MAC** **limit** breaches trigger **sticky** **MAC** syslog storylines unrelated to **EVPN** **mobility** storms—correlate interface **`storm-control`** counters on **access** **leaf** **CLI** before **`dup_mac_investigate`** escalations.

**orchestrator_api_blind_spot** — **OpenStack** **Nova**/**Kubernetes** **API** outages delay ticketing yet **workloads** still migrate—**`orchestrator_api_blind_spot`** explains **`silent`** drift positives until API health dashboards reconcile with Splunk timelines.

**lab_alias_duplicate_injection** — **CI** labs **reuse** **gold** **images** with identical **MACs** on parallel **VNIs**—segregate **`index=lab_fabric`** or tag **`environment=lab`** so training **`multi_vtep_same_mac`** rows never page production bridges.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk technical add-ons: install Splunk Add-on for Cisco IOS/NX-OS (**Splunkbase 2846**), Splunk Add-on for Cisco Nexus (**6876**), Splunk Add-on for Arista EOS (**4495**), and Splunk Add-on for Juniper Networks (**2847**) on heavy forwarders so syslog priorities stay intact. Optional Splunk Connect for Syslog scales multi-vendor fan-in where hundreds of leaves aggregate.
• Structured collectors: schedule Ansible, Nornir, or Python jobs on each data-center leaf to capture only what matters for MAC mobility—EOS uses **`show bgp evpn route-type mac-ip`**, NX-OS uses **`show l2route evpn mac-ip all`**, Junos uses **`show evpn mac-table`**, SONiC uses **`show mac`**, Cumulus uses **`show evpn mac vni all`**. Serialize them into **sourcetype `sdn:evpn:route`** with fields **`mac_address`**, **`vni`**, **`mm_seq`**, **`vtep_ip`**, **`route_action`**, **`leaf_hostname`**, **`fabric_site`** before events hit the indexers.
• Sidecars: land **`sdn:vtep:inventory`** with loopback-to-rack identity, **`sdn:bgp:neighbor`** with AFI 25 / SAFI 70 state for context during storms, and **`sdn:fabric:config`** when overlays add EVIs or rewrite import/export route targets so Splunk can separate knob work from stealth moves.
• Lookups: keep **`sdn_vtep_switch_map.csv`** and **`maintenance_overlay.csv`** in Git so **primary_vtep** from SPL resolves to **site_code** and suppress windows attach cleanly to CMDB tags.
• RBAC: grant the **`fabric_observer`** role search rights on **`dc_fabric`**, **`network_ops`**, **`sdn_ops`**, **`os`**, and **`cisco_dc`** indexes; keep alert-action tokens restricted to the automation account that posts to ticketing.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Type-2 differencing: keep the previous JSON blob per leaf (S3, Redis, or local spool) so you can derive **`nlri_op`** values of **add**, **update**, or **withdraw** without span-tapping raw BGP frames—especially important when NetOps already rate-limit telemetry APIs.
(2) Syslog normalization: ensure NX-OS **`L2RIB-3-L2RIB_THREAD_MAC_MOVE_DETECTED`**, EOS **`EVPN-MACMOVE`**, Junos **`RPD_EVPN_MAC_MOVE`**, and FRR **`MAC move`** strings always hit the same scoping macros. Heavy forwarder **`props.conf`** may clone multiple vendor sourcetypes into the pattern **`arista:eos:syslog`** reference lane or into a shared **`EVENTTYPE=evpn_mac_mobility_syslog`** tag when fields align.
(3) BGP neighbor posture: **`sdn:bgp:neighbor`** must carry BGP state transitions; if moves spike while peers stay **Established**, the story is local dataplane learning, whereas simultaneous resets implicate transport or RR instability (correlate with sibling UC-18.3.4 timelines before rewriting thresholds).
(4) Orchestrator bridges: optional modular inputs ingest vCenter **Task**, OpenStack **InstanceAction**, or Kubernetes **Node** cordon data into **`virt:migration:audit`** for join-based drift detection—Splunk still works without them, but virtualisation teams appreciate fewer false escalations.
(5) Secrets: store API keys and SSH jump credentials in your vault of record; reference them from **passwords.conf** stanzas rotated on a fixed cadence rather than embedding cleartext inside saved searches.

Step 2 — Create the search
Publish saved search **`evpn_mac_mobility_core`** on a five-minute schedule with **`earliest=-4h@m`** for analyst drilldown. Parameterize thresholds through macros **`mac_storm_min_events`** (default **12**), **`mm_seq_alert_floor`** (default **5**), and **`vni_load_hot`** (default **600** moves per five-minute bucket per VNI). Throttle alert deduplication to 25 minutes for the tuple **`mac_norm`** + **`vni_key`** unless **`storm_bucket`** escalates from **`mac_churn_anomaly`** to **`dup_mac_investigate`**, which should break suppression for safety.

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=sdn_ops OR index=os OR index=cisco_dc)
(sourcetype="sdn:evpn:route" OR sourcetype="sdn:bgp:neighbor" OR sourcetype="sdn:vtep:inventory" OR sourcetype="sdn:fabric:config" OR sourcetype="arista:eos:syslog")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval leaf_node=trim(coalesce(leaf_hostname,hostname,switch_name,device_name,host,""))
| eval mac_norm=upper(trim(replace(coalesce(mac_address,client_mac,sticky_mac,dl_src,evpn_mac,""),"[:-]","")))
| eval vni_key=tonumber(coalesce(vni,vxlan_vni,l2_vni,evi_id,evi,""))
| eval mm_seq=tonumber(coalesce(mobility_seq,mm_seq,mac_mobility_seq,seq_num,route_seq,""))
| eval vtep_ip=trim(coalesce(originating_router,originator_ip,nexthop,nexthop_vtep,remote_vtep,vtep_src,nve_src,""))
| eval evt_family=case(
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(sticky|duplicate.?mac|dup.?mac|aliasing)"),"dup_mac_signal",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(L2RIB-3-L2RIB_THREAD_MAC_MOVE|EVPN-MACMOVE|RPD_EVPN_MAC_MOVE|MAC move|mac.?move)"),"syslog_mac_move",
    match(lower(coalesce(route_action,evpn_op,nlri_op,"")),"(?i)withdraw|wdr|purge"),"rt2_withdraw",
    match(lower(coalesce(route_action,evpn_op,nlri_op,"")),"(?i)add|advertise|learn"),"rt2_advertise",
    match(lower(coalesce(route_action,evpn_op,nlri_op,"")),"(?i)update|modify|replace"),"rt2_update",
    sourcetype=="sdn:evpn:route" AND match(lower(coalesce(evpn_route_type,nlri_rt,bgp_evpn_rt,"")),"(?i)type.?2|mac.?ip|^2$"),"rt2_structured",
    null())
| where isnotnull(evt_family) OR mm_seq>0 OR match(lower(_raw),"(?i)type.?2|mac.?ip|l2vpn.?evpn")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count AS mac_evt dc(vtep_ip) AS vtep_dc max(mm_seq) AS seq_top values(vtep_ip) AS vtep_list values(evt_family) AS evt_mix values(leaf_node) AS leaf_list min(_time) AS win_start max(_time) AS win_end BY _time vni_key mac_norm
| eval primary_vtep=mvindex(mvsort(mvdedup(vtep_list)),0)
| eval ownership_spray=if(vtep_dc>1 AND mac_norm!="","multi_vtep_same_mac","single_owner_window")
| eval seq_stress=if(seq_top>=5 OR mac_evt>=25 OR ownership_spray=="multi_vtep_same_mac",1,0)
| eval storm_bucket=case(
    seq_stress=1 AND match(mvjoin(evt_mix,"|"),"(?i)dup_mac_signal"),"dup_mac_investigate",
    seq_stress=1 AND mac_evt>=60,"vm_migration_storm",
    seq_stress=1 AND seq_top>=5,"mobility_seq_runaway",
    seq_stress=1,"mac_churn_anomaly",
    null())
| where isnotnull(storm_bucket)
| eventstats sum(mac_evt) AS vni_churn_sum BY _time vni_key
| eval vni_pressure=if(vni_churn_sum>600,"vni_hot","vni_nominal")
| lookup sdn_vtep_switch_map ip AS primary_vtep OUTPUTNEW site_code platform_role expected_owner
| sort - mac_evt - seq_top - vni_churn_sum
| head 350
| table _time vni_key mac_norm mac_evt vtep_dc seq_top storm_bucket ownership_spray vni_pressure vni_churn_sum evt_mix leaf_list win_start win_end site_code primary_vtep
```
Understanding this SPL — The **`evt_family`** field tags duplicate-mac syslog clues, MAC-move narratives, and structured Type-2 verbs. After **`bin _time span=5m`**, **`stats`** aggregates mac_evt and surfaces distinct VTEP counts as **`vtep_dc`** with the maximum mobility counter **`seq_top`**. When **`vtep_dc`** exceeds one for the same normalized MAC, **`ownership_spray`** becomes **`multi_vtep_same_mac`**, a strong hint for cloning conflicts or split-brain attachment. **`storm_bucket`** collapses severity into **`dup_mac_investigate`**, **`vm_migration_storm`**, **`mobility_seq_runaway`**, or residual **`mac_churn_anomaly`** states for routing pages. **`eventstats`** on **`vni_churn_sum`** powers tenant heat maps; **`vni_pressure`** flags **`vni_hot`** when cross-MAC churn crosses the **`vni_load_hot`** macro. **`primary_vtep`** feeds the **`sdn_vtep_switch_map`** lookup so site_code appears on tables without manual WHOIS lookups.

Step 3 — Validate
(1) Arista: pick a noisy row and compare mac_evt against **`show bgp evpn route-type mac-ip`** output; **`mm_seq`** in Splunk should trail the CLI by at most one polling interval.
(2) Cisco Nexus: when syslog_mac_move floods evt_mix, run **`show l2route evpn mac ip`** variants for that platform train and verify the MAC truly bounced between originating VTEPs rather than reflecting an ARP resync false positive.
(3) Juniper: **`show evpn mac-table`** should echo the same vni_key binding you indexed; if not, field aliases probably mis-map **`evi_id`** versus **`vxlan_vni`**.
(4) SONiC and Cumulus: compare _time against container journal timestamps; bulk docker restart frr events explain temporary lag, not fabric pathology.
(5) Lookup integrity: null site_code after lookup means the CSV drifted—fix inventory before lowering thresholds, otherwise on-call noise skyrockets.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard Studio (or classic XML) should front-load a mac_evt timechart stacked by **`storm_bucket`**, a heatmap of leaf_list versus **`vni_pressure`**, and a sortable table exposing evt_mix, **`primary_vtep`**, and **`vni_churn_sum`**. Escalation policy: **`dup_mac_investigate`** pages virtualization plus NetOps jointly; **`mobility_seq_runaway`** stays with fabric L2 owners; **`vm_migration_storm`** routes through compute NOC when CHG evidence is missing. Archive weekly CSV extracts for capacity governance tying mobility tax to measured control-plane CPU on leaf route processors.

Step 5 — Alert design, visualization tuning, and troubleshoot
Alert design: schedule a correlation search that fires when **`storm_bucket`** is **`dup_mac_investigate`** or **`mobility_seq_runaway`**, or when **`ownership_spray`** equals **`multi_vtep_same_mac`** while mac_evt meets the **`mac_storm_min_events`** macro. Set severity high for duplicate investigations, medium for migration storms lacking change metadata, and informational for benign churn with CHG tokens. Suppress for fifteen minutes when fabric_site appears in **`maintenance_overlay`** with an active chg_id. Include evt_mix, vni_key, **`primary_vtep`**, and deep links to collector Git revision in the payload so responders trust field provenance.
Visualization tuning: limit timechart series to the top twelve VNIs by **`vni_churn_sum`** to avoid unreadable spaghetti; color **`vni_hot`** deep amber, duplicate investigations crimson, and normal churn slate. Add per-row sparklines on tables showing seq_top slopes so executives see runaway counters without opening Search.
Troubleshoot: empty panels while CLI shows moves usually means sourcetype filters omit your vendor lane—run **`| metadata type=sourcetypes`** on the **`dc_fabric`** index and reconcile. Inflated mac_evt may be duplicate syslog TCP taps through redundant relays—use **`dedup`** on host,_time,_cd in diagnostics before raising macros. Missing mm_seq values often mean collectors skip extended-community parsing—enable BGP telemetry templates from vendor guides. After large fabric rebuilds, rebuild baselines because **`vni_load_hot`** defaults assume steady-state tenant counts, not greenfield bursts.
Governance notes: require two NetOps approvers on pull requests that touch this SPL; document collector JSON samples beside the use case wiki page linked to RFC 7432 Section 15.1; mirror **`sdn_vtep_switch_map`** to the disaster-recovery search head cluster; run quarterly tabletop drills injecting synthetic dup_mac_signal traffic; export summarized **`storm_bucket`** counts to a metrics index when legal retention demands multi-year rollups. These habits keep MAC mobility monitoring trustworthy without decorative runbook noise.
grandmaExplanation: We trace when the same guest sticker hops rack to rack faster than your move calendar says it should, and when two racks both insist they own that sticker. That heads off crossed wires before east-west apps feel it.
hardware: **Leaf**/**spine** **BGP-EVPN** fabrics on **Cisco** **Nexus** **9300V**/9500V, **Arista** **7050X3**/7280R3, **Juniper** **QFX5120**/QFX10002, **SONiC** **Enterprise** **distribution** switches, **NVIDIA** **Cumulus** **VX** **5.x** hosts acting as **VTEPs** with **FRR** **EVPN**.
